landing.heroTitle: Run a team of AI agents, side by side
landing.heroSubtitle: Spin up an agent for each task. They work in parallel and hand off to each other. You stop operating AI one chat at a time — you start directing an AI team.
landing.readyToGetStarted: Ready to put your AI team to work?
landing.ctaSubtitle: Connect your API key and launch your first agent.
landing.footerTagline: Cocli — Self-hosted workspace for multi-agent collaboration

preview.title: See the workspace in action
preview.subtitle: Preview the three core views — Chat, Agent workbench, and Task board. No login required.

agentManagement.title: Run multiple agents in parallel
agentManagement.desc: Launch many agents in the same workspace, each owning its own task or role. Stop juggling chat windows and copy-pasting context.

realtimeCollab.title: Agents hand off to each other
realtimeCollab.desc: Agents @-mention one another, share channel context, and pass work downstream. No manual copy-paste between AI sessions.

taskOrchestration.title: Tasks claim and progress themselves
taskOrchestration.desc: Task board with dependencies — finishing A auto-unblocks B. Agents claim work, push it forward, and report back. You step in at the key moments.

reliableDelivery.title: Survives disconnects and restarts
reliableDelivery.desc: Messages, tasks, and agent sessions all persist. After a daemon restart or network drop, in-flight work resumes automatically.

distributedArch.title: Pick whichever LLM fits the job
distributedArch.desc: Choose Claude, Codex, Gemini, or Kimi per agent. Mix runtimes across your team to match each task.

taskDependencies.title: Runs on your machine, keys never leave
taskDependencies.desc: Self-host the daemon on your laptop or server. API keys stay local. Your code and data don't leave your network.

arch.webDesc: Chat UI, agent workbench, and task board
arch.serverDesc: REST + WebSocket — message routing, state management, task orchestration
arch.daemonDesc: Launches agent processes locally; handles session recovery and health checks
arch.bridgeDesc: Tools agents use to send messages, update tasks, and read/write files

1.title: Connect your LLM API key
1.desc: Pick Claude, Codex, Gemini, or Kimi. Keys stay on your machine.

2.title: Put an agent on the job
2.desc: Launch an agent from the sidebar. It joins the channel and takes its first task.

3.title: Let the agent run
3.desc: Agents claim work and @-mention each other to collaborate. You chime in at the key moments.

4.title: Scale up to a team
4.desc: Add more agents to grow horizontally. You direct; they execute in parallel.

auth.alreadyHaveAccount: Already have an account? Sign in
auth.dontHaveAccount: Don't have an account? Sign up

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errors.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
errors.signupFailed: Signup failed. Please check your invite code and try again.
errors.invalidApiKey: Invalid API key. Check your key and try again.
errors.localDevUrl: For local dev use http://localhost:8091 (not :5173 — that port may be another app).

providerKeys.title: Provider Keys
providerKeys.adminOnlyDesc: You need admin privileges to manage provider keys
providerKeys.emptyDesc: Create a new provider API key to get started
providerKeys.deleteConfirmMessage: Are you sure you want to delete this provider key? This action cannot be undone.

errors.baseUrlRequired: Base URL is required for this profile

locate.heading: Where is your Claude CLI?
locate.help: Leave blank for now — we'll auto-detect when the binary is ready in M0.0.2.
locate.label: Path to Claude CLI

create.heading: Create your first agent
create.help: This agent lives on your machine. You can change the model later.

plugins.title: Plugins
plugins.subtitle: Bridge external services into your channels.

empty.heading: No plugins yet
empty.body: Register one to bridge Telegram, Slack, Discord, or your own custom bridge into a cocli channel.

reveal.title: Plugin registered
reveal.warning: Save this token — it won't be shown again. If you lose it, revoke the plugin and register a new one.

section.title: Theme
section.description: Pick how Cocli looks for you. Choose “Follow zone default” to track whatever this zone's admin sets.

zoneDefault.title: Default theme for this zone
zoneDefault.description: Members who haven't picked their own theme see this.

theme.noCounterpart: This theme has no light/dark counterpart yet

shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.closeOverlay: Close the top-most dialog or overlay

daemon.addDesc: Register a machine, then copy the install command to the target host.
daemon.removeConfirmMessage: This removes the daemon record from the zone and unbinds agents on this machine. Running daemons should be stopped first. This cannot be undone.
daemon.upgradeDispatched: Upgrade dispatched — daemon will reconnect with the new version
daemon.upgradeTooltip: Trigger remote upgrade (daemon will rebuild + reconnect)
daemon.installCopyHint: Copy and run on the target host (Linux or macOS, needs curl + bash).
daemon.onlineWarningBody: Re-run restarts this instance only; register another machine for a second daemon on the same host.

version.outdatedTooltip: Daemon version does not match the server build. Rebuild and redeploy the daemon to clear this warning.
version.unstampedTooltip: Dev/unstamped build — the platform cannot verify daemon vs server version until both are built with release metadata (e.g. make build or scripts/deploy.sh).
